threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's 'lovely' when someone talks about the tube strikes. Wait till you’ve waited an hour for a bus in the rain.
Saw a couple arguing about the merits of chicken shops vs. curry houses on the 21 last night. Classic Peckham debate at 2 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a family picnic on a patch of grass while a street musician played tuba—only in Neukölln do these oddities blend so beautifully.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Friedrichshain, it's common to find locals hosting tech meetups in old warehouses. The mix of coding and club vibes is uniquely Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they don’t flinch at BART being evacuated for smoke; just another Tuesday in San Francisco.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 38 at Civic Center today—passenger on the phone arguing with their dog about getting off. Classic San Francisco chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí; they miss the quiet corners of Carrer de Verdi where locals sip coffee and catch up.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their reluctance to try the local bakeries; they always pick the chain spots instead. Missing out on the warm croissants from down the street!

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still queue for the bus instead of dodging in and out of the double-decker like a pro. The locals know it's a contact sport.
Saw a guy on the 59 last night singing nursery rhymes to a toddler—toddler was unimpressed, but the whole bus was in stitches.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I walked past the Wochenmarkt and heard a spontaneous jam session with an accordionist and a beatboxer—only in Neukölln do these things just happen.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a graffiti-covered kebab shop open at 3 AM, serving the best late-night döners while the world spins around you.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a local would know to sidestep the Civic Center during rush hour, especially with the BART drama—smoke and chaos is just a Tuesday here.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a couple arguing over a seat on the 38 Geary, ended with one dramatically storming off at Civic Center. Muni’s a stage, isn't it?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's square is always buzzing, but locals know it quiets down post-10 PM. That's when the real conversations happen in the corners.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their stunned reaction to the lack of tap water in bars; locals just order a cervesa instead.
